subject: [RFC PATCH v3 0/4] mm/zsmalloc: per-cpu deferred free to accelerate swap entry release

reviews.author: Wenchao Hao
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the current size of the deferred metadata (256K) may not scale well with large memory sizes or number of CPUs, and suggested parameterizing this value to make it more flexible.

Reviewer questioned the specificity and scalability of the patch's logic, noting that it may not be suitable for systems with a large number of CPUs or memory sizes.
reviews.raw_body:
256K holds the deferred metadata for ~128MB zswap or ~256MB zram
entries, which matches what a killed process typically has swapped
out. Pages sitting in the pool are memory that can't be used
elsewhere, so I didn't want it to grow with RAM/CPU. Happy to
parameterize it if you'd prefer.

---

Will replace with ZS_PUSH_FULL_QUEUED, if v4 still has
this logic.

Thanks,
Wenchao

2026-05-09.patch_summary: The patch introduces a deferred free framework for zsmalloc, which allows for more efficient handling of large amounts of memory by delaying the actual freeing of memory until it's safe to do so. This is particularly useful in situations where there are many CPUs or large amounts of memory involved, as it helps mitigate issues related to concurrent access and fragmentation. The approach taken involves adding callback operations to manage the deferred free process.
